location.office_time_required: Flex First — employees choose where they work; regular in-person events

secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with enterprise GTM / B2B platform focus

level_signals: 8+ years of B2B product management or similar experience
2+ years of direct involvement with sales or consulting and customer discovery with senior executives
Base pay range $184K–$233K depending on state, consistent with Senior PM
Role described as 'one of the most dynamic and high leverage roles we have'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of B2B product management or similar experience, 3+ in ecommerce / retail technology
2+ years of direct involvement with sales or consulting and customer discovery with senior executives
Strong experience operating at the intersection of product and go-to-market, working closely with enterprise sales, marketing, and customer success.
Proven ability to build trust with executives, internally and externally.
Strong leadership skills — able to guide large, multidisciplinary teams toward shared outcomes across Product, Engineering, Data, Design, and GTM.
Demonstrated ability to handle complex product problems across a wide product surface with high ambiguity.

Strong product vision and communication skills — able to envision how grocery and e-commerce will evolve over the next three years, translate near-term asks into scalable platform offerings, and layer in emerging technologies to go beyond incremental improvements.

Proven customer and product strategy ability:
a) Build trust with external customers
b) Identify needs and craft complex platform solutions
c) Rally internal teams to make high-stakes tradeoffs under ambiguity and tight deadlines
Fluent in both product intuition and data: able to assess value propositions for both retailers and their end customers, balancing qualitative insights and quantitative impact.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in creative tools, content management systems, or site building in a B2B context

scope.surface: White-label retailer ecommerce platform — site theming & brand platform, digital asset management, enterprise retailer GTM and partnership
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS white-label ecommerce platform for enterprise and mid-market retailers
primary_metrics_inferred: Retailer ecommerce conversion and brand experience quality
Enterprise deal wins and pipeline influence
core_responsibilities: Own the long term vision, strategy, and roadmap for site theming and content management
Build a centralized platform where retailers configure and manage the visual representation of their sites
Own the Digital Asset Manager (DAM): asset hosting, uploading, management, brand rules enforcement, and an API
Define the right balance between retailer flexibility and platform coherence
Shape the long-term vision for how AI can augment page editing and merchandising workflows
Enable and influence multiple product teams to build towards multi year horizons
Regularly represent Instacart externally to the world's largest retailers as an innovation partner
Drive cross-functional alignment on complex retailer requirements to win deals and support retailer-driven innovation roadmaps
Partner closely with Sales and Marketing to shape collateral and narratives that drive enterprise go-to-market success

compensation.equity_form: New hire equity grant plus annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation ranges explicitly disclosed across multiple geographies ($184K–$233K base)
Equity (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Clear dual product surface with well-defined ownership (site theming + enterprise GTM)
High-leverage, senior role with direct external retailer exposure
AI roadmap component signals forward-looking product investment
red_flags: Exceptionally broad scope — site theming, DAM, enterprise GTM, and retailer partnership all in one role — may signal under-staffing or scope creep risk
8+ years minimum experience plus 3+ years ecommerce and 2+ years sales/consulting creates a narrow candidate profile
Role requires fluency across both deep platform/technical product work and enterprise sales dynamics simultaneously, which is a rare combination
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization — unclear for non-US candidates despite remote-global hashtag not being present

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + compensation range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd min/max (stated as ranges per geography; min = lowest state tier floor, max = CA/NY/CT/NJ ceiling)
ambiguities: Base pay range spans $184K–$233K across US geographies; the single base_range_usd field captures the full spread (lowest floor to highest ceiling) across all listed state tiers
JD uses #LI-Remote tag and describes Flex First policy but does not explicitly confirm whether international remote candidates are eligible; treated as remote-us only
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; marked as 'unclear'
